report_name: Chinese APT 'Webworm' Uses Discord and MS Graph API for C2 in New Backdoor Attacks

threat_actors.name: Webworm
threat_actors.description: A China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group active since at least 2022, known for deploying custom backdoors using legitimate services for C2. It has historically targeted government, IT, aerospace, and energy sectors in Asia and Russia, and has recently expanded its targeting to Europe and Africa.
threat_actors.goals: Intelligence gathering, targeting state secrets, and intellectual property.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The EchoCreep and GraphWorm backdoors use HTTPS to communicate with the Discord and Microsoft Graph APIs for command and control.

attack_patterns.name: Bidirectional Communication
attack_patterns.description: The malware uses legitimate web services (Discord and Microsoft Graph API) for two-way C2 communication, receiving commands and exfiltrating data.

malware.name: EchoCreep
malware.description: A custom backdoor deployed by the Webworm APT group that uses the Discord API for command-and-control communications.

malware.name: GraphWorm
malware.description: A custom backdoor deployed by the Webworm APT group that leverages the Microsoft Graph API for command-and-control, allowing its traffic to blend with legitimate Microsoft 365 activity.

infrastructure.name: Discord C2 Channel
infrastructure.description: The Discord API was abused by the EchoCreep backdoor to receive commands and exfiltrate data, masquerading as legitimate traffic.

infrastructure.name: Microsoft Graph API C2 Channel
infrastructure.description: The Microsoft Graph API was abused by the GraphWorm backdoor for C2 and data exfiltration, blending in with normal Microsoft 365 traffic.

indicators.name: Webworm C2 Domain Communication
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Anomalous Process Connecting to Discord or MS Graph
id: 7f5b1a3d-8c4e-4f9a-b2d1-0e6c7d8f9a0b
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to Discord or Microsoft Graph domains from non-standard processes. This could indicate C2 activity by malware like Webworm's backdoors.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/webworm-deploys-echocreep-and.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.command_and_control
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationHostname|endswith:
      - 'discord.com'
      - 'graph.microsoft.com'
    Initiated: true
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\Discord.exe'
      - '\teams.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\msteams.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
level: medium

indicators.name: Suspicious Process Accessing Discord API
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Process Connecting to Discord API
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to discord.com from processes that are not typical web browsers or the Discord client itself. This is a hunting query for backdoors like EchoCreep.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/webworm-deploys-echocreep-and.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.command_and_control
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationHostname|endswith: '.discord.com'
    Initiated: true
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Discord.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\iexplore.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
level: medium

indicators.name: High-Risk Microsoft Graph API Permissions Granted
indicators.pattern:
title: High-Risk Microsoft Graph API Permissions Granted
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4f-3e2d-1c0b-9a8b7c6d5e4f
status: experimental
description: Monitors for applications being granted high-risk permissions to the Microsoft Graph API, such as Mail.ReadWrite or Files.ReadWrite.All. This could indicate C2 setup by malware like GraphWorm.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/webworm-deploys-echocreep-and.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1098.003
  - attack.persistence
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.azuread
detection:
  selection:
    Operation: 'Add app role assignment to service principal'
    TargetResources|contains:
      - 'DisplayName: Mail.ReadWrite'
      - 'DisplayName: Files.ReadWrite.All'
  condition: selection
level: high

relationships.source_name: High-Risk Microsoft Graph API Permissions Granted
